title: gagea pygmaea subsp. nevadensis
intro: Gagea pygmaea subsp. nevadensis, also known as the Nevada gagea, is a rare and endemic subspecies of flowering plant in the lily family. It is a small, bulbous perennial with white or pale yellow flowers and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant is said to possess anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds. Herbal preparations of Gagea pygmaea subsp. nevadensis include infusions and decoctions, which can be made by boiling the roots or flowers in water to create a tea for potential health benefits.
